title: systemd user context issue
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create new container as non-root user, run: `podman generate systemd --name --new rootless-nginx -f` , exec `systemctl --user daemon-reload`

2. start the service with `systemctl --user start container-rootless-nginx`



**Describe the results you received:**

podman systemd service fails with:

> Jan 14 22:14:38 localhost.localdomain systemd[1666]: container-rootless-nginx.service: New main PID 29453 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Jan 14 22:14:38 localhost.localdomain systemd[1666]: container-rootless-nginx.service: New main PID 29453 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.



**Describe the results you expected:**

A running nginx server running rootless


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0

      host_id: 100000

  kernel: 5.10.6-200.fc33.x86_64

      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true

    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 4294963200
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Phsyical Fedora 33 machine, the same setup works fine with CentOS and another Fedora 33 machine I run

comments.body: Please paste the content of the service file. Did you create the container with `--userns keep-id` or a custom uid mapping?

I create the container with ansible (it is a demo for running a container rootless)

> - name: create nginx container
  containers.podman.podman_container:
    name: rootless-nginx
    image: docker.io/library/nginx
    image_strict: no
    state: present
    publish: 80:80
    volume:
            - "{{ podman_volume_path }}:/usr/share/nginx/html:Z"
  become_user: "{{ podman_user }}"

For testing purposes I tried to create the systemd unit files with the `--new` option and without.
Both with the same error.

Here are the files:

**created without --new**

> [Unit]
Description=Podman container-rootless-nginx.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start rootless-nginx
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 rootless-nginx
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 rootless-nginx
PIDFile=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/4664e43607d00a006f548efc440379ca5fcf74e3b2a0c74d4a1f8b9ddad665c0/userdata/conmon.pid
KillMode=none
Type=forking
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

**created with --new option**

> [Unit]
Description=Podman container-rootless-nginx.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/container-rootless-nginx.pid %t/container-rootless-nginx.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/container-rootless-nginx.pid --cidfile %t/container-rootless-nginx.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon -d --replace --name rootless-nginx --publish 80:80 --volume /home/dan/work/rootless-podman-demo/roles/rootless-podman-demo/files:/usr/share/nginx/html:Z --detach=True docker.io/library/nginx
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile %t/container-rootless-nginx.ctr-id -t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f --cidfile %t/container-rootless-nginx.ctr-id
PIDFile=%t/container-rootless-nginx.pid
KillMode=none
Type=forking
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

I just set up another VM with Fedora 33 and Podman, well...setup is similar to my workstation, non-root user creating container, generate systemd unit file...works as expected - any suggestions where else I could take a look?
Where did you put the service file?
does it work better if you try `--cgroups=split` instead of `--cgroups=no-conmon` in the ExecStart directive?
Nope, this does nothing. The container itself is running fine rootless, only the managment via systemd does not work on my machine.
any further ideas where to take a look?
Are you starting the files via ansible?
I only saw this issue in https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4420581 or when setting the `User` attribute in a systemd unit running Podman (which is not supported).
I am going to close since I am convinced it's a systemd issue. But we can continue the conversation here.

> Are you starting the files via ansible?

I think you mean "generate" them? Yes, with the shell module and becoming the user who should run the service. But it also fails when I create them manually. Doesn't matter though :/

> I only saw this issue in https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4420581 or when setting the User attribute in a systemd unit running Podman (which is not supported).

I already found that which did not help in my case.

> I am going to close since I am convinced it's a systemd issue. But we can continue the conversation here.

Thank you for helping me :)

_________

As i mentioned earlier in this thread, I already tried a fresh install of Fedora 33 and that did work as expected. So I think my system is kinda messed up, but I am not sure where to look. I already tried to disable SELinux at a first step with no luck.
Can you try removing `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf` and re-run? Or did you run on a fresh system?

I removed the file and when I try to `systemctl --user start mycontainer.service` the commando hangs and then times out
But `systemctl --user status mycontainer.service` shows the following:

`Feb 09 21:19:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1690]: Starting Podman container-httpd.service...
Feb 09 21:19:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1690]: con-httpd.service: Found left-over process 15596 (podman pause) in control group while starting unit. Ignoring.
Feb 09 21:19:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1690]: This usually indicates unclean termination of a previous run, or service implementation deficiencies.
Feb 09 21:19:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1690]: con-httpd.service: New main PID 16300 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Feb 09 21:19:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1690]: con-httpd.service: New main PID 16300 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.`

The container is running somehow:

`CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND           CREATED         STATUS             PORTS               NAMES
5b3923d7e9b1  docker.io/library/httpd:latest  httpd-foreground  45 seconds ago  Up 45 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  httpd`

But the systemd service is still failing
@msekletar, do you have an idea how the upper error can occur? It seems to only occur on this specific machine.

Hi,
I know that this conversation is old but I'have exactly the same problem.
Have you find something ?
